target.title: fix(telegram): recover lone timed-out spool handlers
target.body:
## Summary

- Include active Telegram isolated-ingress handlers in timeout recovery candidates, even when no later same-lane update is queued.
- Keep timed-out active handlers marked as stalled until they settle or are recovered.
- Add regression coverage for a lone `.json.processing` topic update timing out without backlog.

Fixes #84158.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
A lone active Telegram isolated-ingress `.json.processing` claim could be missed by timeout recovery when no later same-lane update existed to populate `blockedByLane`.

Real environment tested:
Local macOS checkout at commit `43c2a0c7b1`, using the real `TelegramPollingSession`, Telegram ingress spool, reply fence, and isolated-ingress drain/recovery code with a local mock Telegram bot runtime and no network calls.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
`PATH=/Users/andy/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/node/bin:$PATH node --import tsx /private/tmp/proof-84158.mjs`

Evidence after fix:
Terminal output from the live isolated-ingress spool proof:
```text
openclaw-telegram-lone-spooled-timeout-proof=ok
events=bot0:42
bot_instances=2
pending_update_ids=none
failed_update_ids=42
timeout_log_present=true
```

Observed result after fix:
The single claimed topic update `42` timed out without any later same-lane backlog, was written as a failed tombstone, left no pending updates behind, logged the timeout, and restarted isolated ingress.

What was not tested:
I did not connect to the live Telegram Bot API. The proof avoids external network calls and exercises the local spool/recovery behavior directly.

## Validation

- `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 PATH=/Users/andy/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/node/bin:$PATH node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts --pool forks --maxWorkers 1 --vmMemoryLimit 8192MB` - 43 passed
- `PATH=/Users/andy/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/node/bin:$PATH node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts`
- `PATH=/Users/andy/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/node/bin:$PATH node_modules/.bin/oxlint extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `git log --format='%h %an <%ae> %s' upstream/main..HEAD`:
  `43c2a0c7b1 Andy Ye <35905412+TurboTheTurtle@users.noreply.github.com> fix(telegram): recover lone timed-out spool handlers`

target.title: fix: allow provider timeout overlays
target.body:
## Summary

- Allows `models.providers.<id>.timeoutSeconds` and other provider-level tuning to be used as overlays without forcing a full custom provider declaration.
- Keeps custom provider declarations strict: setting `baseUrl` or `models` still requires the matching full provider declaration shape.
- Prevents overlay-only provider entries from synthesizing fallback models for typoed or unknown model refs.
- Guards Microsoft Foundry provider selection against overlay-only provider config.

Closes #83201.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/config/schema.test.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/model.test.ts extensions/microsoft-foundry/index.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/tsgo -p tsconfig.core.json --noEmit --incremental false --pretty false`
- `node_modules/.bin/tsgo -p tsconfig.extensions.json --noEmit --incremental false --pretty false`
- `codex review --uncommitted`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Provider-level timeout overlays can be set without declaring custom models, while overlay-only entries no longer cause unknown model refs to resolve as synthetic fallback models.

Real environment tested: WSL-native checkout at `origin/main` `b86435f0b5`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran format, `git diff --check`, focused Vitest for config/model/Foundry coverage, core and extension `tsgo`, then `codex review --uncommitted` in `/root/openclaw-wsl-provider-83201`.

Evidence after fix: Focused Vitest passed 6 files / 353 tests; core `tsgo` passed; extension `tsgo` passed; Codex review reported no actionable correctness issues.

Observed result after fix: Timeout-only overlays apply to discovered provider models; typoed overlay-only providers and built-in `api` overlays return `Unknown model` instead of fabricating a fallback model.

What was not tested: Full CI was not run locally; this was scoped to the affected config, model resolution, and Microsoft Foundry provider surfaces.

target.title: fix(twitch): guard onMessage cleanup against newer handler (#83888)
target.body:
Fixes #83888.

`TwitchClientManager.onMessage` (`extensions/twitch/src/twitch-client.ts:197`) registers a handler under `getAccountKey(account)` and returns a cleanup closure that captures `key`. The closure calls `this.messageHandlers.delete(key)` unconditionally. If a second `onMessage(account, h2)` call overwrites the first handler — which `messageHandlers.set` silently does — and the first caller's cleanup then fires, it evicts the live `h2`. The account is left with no handler and the next inbound chat message — and every one after — is silently dropped. The bug is invisible to existing tests because none register two handlers for the same account key and then exercise the first cleanup.

## Changes

- `extensions/twitch/src/twitch-client.ts`: guard the cleanup closure with `if (this.messageHandlers.get(key) === handler) { this.messageHandlers.delete(key); }`. The lone-handler case is unchanged; the only difference is that a stale cleanup is now a no-op instead of yanking a newer handler out from under a second caller.
- `extensions/twitch/src/twitch-client.test.ts`: two new regression cases under the existing `describe("onMessage", …)` block. (i) Register `h1` → register `h2` (overwrites) → invoke `cleanup1` → assert `h2` is still the registered handler. (ii) Register `h_solo` → invoke `cleanup_solo` → assert the entry is removed (no regression on the single-handler path).

Diff stat: 2 files, +32 / -1.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `messageHandlers` is the per-key `Map<string, (msg) => void>` at `twitch-client.ts:~70-80`; `getAccountKey(account)` derives a stable `{bot}:{channel}` string used for both `set` and the captured cleanup `key`. The new identity check makes the cleanup closure idempotent w.r.t. unrelated overwrites — only the exact registered handler is removed.

- **Real environment tested:** Local Node 22.x. Probe at `/tmp/probe_83888.mjs` does both halves of the proof. (a) Parses the patched `twitch-client.ts` and verifies the cleanup closure contains `this.messageHandlers.get(key) === handler` and that the `set(key, handler)` precedes the guarded cleanup. (b) Replays the buggy and patched shapes in pure JS — registers two handlers for the same key, invokes the first cleanup, dispatches a message, and asserts: pre-fix shape silently drops the dispatch (confirms #83888's exact symptom: "Inbound messages are silently dropped"), patched shape preserves the second handler and delivers the dispatch, and the single-handler regression case still removes the lone entry on cleanup.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node /tmp/probe_83888.mjs`

- **Evidence after fix:**

```
PASS: cleanup closure guards delete with identity check (this.messageHandlers.get(key) === handler)
PASS: handler is registered via set() before the guarded cleanup closure is returned
PASS: replay (buggy): cleanup1 deleted the live handler — message silently dropped (confirms #83888)
PASS: replay (patched): cleanup1 saw mismatched handler and skipped — h2 still receives dispatch
PASS: replay (patched, sole handler): cleanup of the only registered handler still removes it — no regression

ALL CASES PASS
```

- **Observed result after fix:** Stale cleanups from earlier `onMessage` registrations are now no-ops when a newer handler is in place. Inbound chat messages for the account continue to reach the newest registered handler. Single-handler unregister behavior is unchanged.

- **What was not tested:** Live `tmi.js` Twitch chat connection — that requires an OAuth token + channel and is outside the scope of a handler-registration race fix. The new vitest cases drive the exact same public boundary the original test (`"should replace existing handler for same account"`) uses, plus the cleanup invocation and a dispatch-equivalent identity assertion.

## Audit (per CLAUDE rules — all 5 steps)

- **Existing-helper check:** No helper is needed for a `Map.get(key) === value` identity check — this is the canonical pattern for handler-table deregistration. PASS
- **Shared-helper caller check:** `onMessage` has one production caller (`extensions/twitch/src/monitor.ts:266`, `monitorTwitchProvider`'s `unregisterHandler = clientManager.onMessage(...)`) plus six test sites. The new behavior is strictly a superset: same outcome on a single-handler timeline, no eviction of a newer handler on stale cleanup. PASS
- **Broader-fix rival scan:** `gh pr list --search '83888 in:title,body'` and `gh pr list --search 'onMessage cleanup in:title,body'` return no open PRs. PASS
- **Recent-merge audit:** `git log --oneline -5 -- extensions/twitch/src/twitch-client.ts` shows `4f4d108639 chore(lint): remove underscore-dangle allow list (#83542)` and `e1061a8b46 test(live): tolerate provider drift in release checks` — unrelated to handler registration. PASS
- **Prototype-pollution scan:** N/A — single `Map.get` + `Map.delete` call.

target.title: Expose messageId in message CLI JSON output
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a top-level `messageId` to `openclaw message send --json` output when the provider receipt contains one.

## Motivation

Closes #84186.

The raw provider payload remains useful, but automation should not need provider-specific knowledge to find the id of the message it just sent.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] CLI output improvement

## Scope

- Extracts `messageId` from direct and nested send receipt shapes.
- Adds top-level `messageId` only when present.
- Preserves the existing raw `payload` object unchanged.
- Adds a focused CLI JSON test for nested receipt output.

## Linked Issue/PR

Closes #84186.

## Real behavior proof

Focused automated proof exercises the CLI JSON path with a nested provider receipt and verifies the top-level `messageId` is emitted while the raw payload is unchanged.

## Root Cause

`buildMessageCliJson` returned the raw provider payload but did not normalize common receipt identifiers into a stable CLI field.

## Regression Test Plan

- `pnpm exec vitest run src/commands/message.test.ts`

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`openclaw message send --json` may now include `messageId` at the top level when available. Existing consumers can continue reading `payload`.

## Diagram

N/A. This is a CLI JSON shape improvement.

## Security Impact

No secrets, new permissions, or external calls. The id is already present in the existing payload.

## Repro + Verification

Before this change, callers had to inspect nested provider payload fields. The new test verifies a nested `payload.result.messageId` becomes top-level `messageId`.

## Evidence

Focused test command passes.

## Human Verification

Not performed against a live provider; the CLI JSON formatter path is covered directly.

## Compatibility / Migration

Backward compatible. This only adds a field when it can be derived.

## Risks

Low. Existing output fields are preserved.

target.title: Allow Discord component registry TTL override
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a bounded Discord `agentComponents.ttlMs` config option and passes it into component registry registration for component sends and edits.

## Motivation

Closes #84184.

Discord component registry registration already accepts a TTL, but normal sends and edits always used the fixed default. Long-running review workflows need an explicit, bounded way to keep callbacks available longer without changing the default for everyone.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Configuration

## Scope

- Adds `agentComponents.ttlMs` to Discord config types and schema.
- Bounds TTL values to positive integers up to seven days.
- Adds a UI hint for the setting.
- Passes the resolved account TTL into sent and edited component registration.
- Adds config validation and send-path propagation coverage.

## Linked Issue/PR

Closes #84184.

## Real behavior proof

Focused automated proof covers both contract and runtime propagation: config parsing accepts bounded TTL values and the send path passes the configured TTL to component registration.

## Root Cause

The registry layer had TTL support, but the Discord account config and send/edit paths did not expose or forward a configured TTL.

## Regression Test Plan

- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/discord/src/send.components.test.ts`

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Discord users can configure `agentComponents.ttlMs`; unset configurations keep the existing 30-minute default.

## Diagram

N/A. This is a config-to-registration plumbing change.

## Security Impact

No new secrets or permissions. Longer TTLs are explicit and bounded to seven days.

## Repro + Verification

Before this change, there was no supported config field to override the registry TTL. The new tests verify accepted/rejected config values and confirm a configured value reaches registration.

## Evidence

Focused test command passes.

## Human Verification

Not performed against a live Discord service; the send-path test asserts the exact registration argument.

## Compatibility / Migration

Backward compatible. Defaults are unchanged when `agentComponents.ttlMs` is unset.

## Risks

Low to medium. Longer callback windows can be useful but should be chosen deliberately, so the schema enforces a bounded positive integer.

target.title: Preserve disabled Discord presentation buttons
target.body:
## Summary

Accepts `disabled` on portable presentation buttons and preserves it in Discord presentation button specs.

## Motivation

Closes #84183.

The runtime type already included `disabled`, and Discord button specs support disabled buttons, but the message tool schema and Discord presentation mapper dropped the field.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- Adds `disabled` to the message tool presentation button schema.
- Copies `disabled: true` into Discord button specs for presentation and legacy interactive button mapping.
- Adds a focused test for disabled presentation button preservation.

## Linked Issue/PR

Closes #84183.

## Real behavior proof

Focused automated proof covers the deterministic mapper: a disabled presentation button now produces a Discord component button with `disabled: true`.

## Root Cause

The portable button type and low-level Discord builder already supported disabled buttons, but the schema and adapter layer did not propagate that field.

## Regression Test Plan

- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/discord/src/shared-interactive.test.ts`

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Callers can send disabled Discord presentation buttons through the message tool without falling back to Discord-specific payloads.

## Diagram

N/A. This is a field propagation fix.

## Security Impact

No new secrets or external calls. The change reduces accidental action availability by preserving an explicit disabled state.

## Repro + Verification

Before this change, `disabled: true` was rejected or dropped before Discord rendering. The new test verifies the generated component spec retains it.

## Evidence

Focused test command passes.

## Human Verification

Not performed against a live Discord service; the change is limited to deterministic schema and mapping behavior.

## Compatibility / Migration

Backward compatible. Existing messages render the same unless they set `disabled: true`.

## Risks

Low. Only true values are propagated.

target.title: fix(twitch): register chat intent for refreshing auth
target.body:
Fixes #83727

## Summary
- Register Twurple's `chat` intent when the Twitch channel adds a refreshing user token.
- Add regression coverage that pins the `addUserForToken(..., ["chat"])` contract.

## Root Cause
The Twitch client used `RefreshingAuthProvider.addUserForToken(token)` without an intent list. Twurple's `ChatClient` queries the auth provider for intent `chat`, so refreshing-token accounts had no known chat intent and failed before a stable IRC connection could be established.

## Validation
- `PATH=/Users/andy/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/node/bin:$PATH node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/twitch/src/twitch-client.test.ts` -> 1 file, 31 passed.
- `PATH=/Users/andy/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/node/bin:$PATH node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/twitch/src/probe.test.ts extensions/twitch/src/twitch-client.test.ts extensions/twitch/src/status.test.ts extensions/twitch/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/twitch/src/plugin.test.ts extensions/twitch/src/plugin.lifecycle.test.ts extensions/twitch/src/send.test.ts extensions/twitch/src/token.test.ts extensions/twitch/src/actions.test.ts extensions/twitch/src/outbound.test.ts extensions/twitch/src/setup-surface.test.ts extensions/twitch/src/plugin.live.test.ts extensions/twitch/src/access-control.test.ts extensions/twitch/src/config.test.ts` -> 13 files, 166 passed.
- `PATH=/Users/andy/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/node/bin:$PATH node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/twitch/src/twitch-client.ts extensions/twitch/src/twitch-client.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `git log --format='%h %an <%ae> %s' upstream/main..HEAD` -> `237216de3f Andy Ye <35905412+TurboTheTurtle@users.noreply.github.com> fix(twitch): register chat intent for refreshing auth`

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
Refreshing Twitch accounts did not bind their token to Twurple's `chat` intent, so Twurple could not retrieve a chat token for the connection path.

Real environment tested:
Local OpenClaw checkout on this branch, bundled Node.js, and the repository's real `@twurple/auth` 8.1.4 package from `node_modules`. No mocked Twurple provider was used in the direct proof.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```shell
PATH=/Users/andy/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/node/bin:$PATH node --input-type=module -e 'import { RefreshingAuthProvider } from "@twurple/auth"; const token={accessToken:"proof-access",refreshToken:"proof-refresh",scope:["chat:read","chat:edit"],expiresIn:3600,obtainmentTimestamp:Date.now()}; const before=new RefreshingAuthProvider({clientId:"proof-client",clientSecret:"proof-secret"}); before.addUser("123456", token); const beforeToken=await before.getAccessTokenForIntent("chat", ["chat:read","chat:edit"]); const after=new RefreshingAuthProvider({clientId:"proof-client",clientSecret:"proof-secret"}); after.addUser("123456", token, ["chat"]); const afterToken=await after.getAccessTokenForIntent("chat", ["chat:read","chat:edit"]); console.log(JSON.stringify({before: beforeToken ?? null, after:{userId: afterToken?.userId, accessToken: afterToken?.accessToken, scopes: afterToken?.scope}, afterIntents: after.getIntentsForUser("123456")}, null, 2));'
```

Evidence after fix:
```json
{
  "before": null,
  "after": {
    "userId": "123456",
    "accessToken": "proof-access",
    "scopes": [
      "chat:read",
      "chat:edit"
    ]
  },
  "afterIntents": [
    "chat"
  ]
}
```

Observed result after fix:
With `["chat"]` registered, Twurple resolves `getAccessTokenForIntent("chat", ["chat:read", "chat:edit"])` to the configured user token instead of returning `null`. The OpenClaw Twitch manager now passes that same intent list to `addUserForToken`, and the regression test asserts the exact call.

What was not tested:
I did not run a live Twitch IRC connection because no live Twitch credentials were available in this environment.

target.title: fix(anthropic): preserve configured Claude image capability
target.body:
## Summary
- normalize stale text-only Anthropic/Claude 4 catalog rows to text+image in the core configured model input path
- keep configured/default `models list` rows aligned with the same Anthropic Claude 4 image-capability normalization
- cover `claude-sonnet-4-5` configured/local catalog behavior while preserving text-only older Claude rows
- add changelog entry

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior addressed:** A configured default model such as `agents.defaults.model = "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5"` could still resolve through a stale text-only configured/catalog path, causing image input to be treated as unsupported even after the provider-discovered row fix.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, PR head `be8789b2e8`, using an isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME=/private/tmp/openclaw-83756-proof` and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/private/tmp/openclaw-83756-proof/.openclaw/openclaw.json` with only `agents.defaults.model` set to `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5`.
- **Exact steps or command run after the patch:**
  ```sh
  env OPENCLAW_HOME=/private/tmp/openclaw-83756-proof \
    OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/private/tmp/openclaw-83756-proof/.openclaw/openclaw.json \
    PATH=/Users/andy/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/node/bin:/opt/homebrew/bin:/usr/bin:/bin:/usr/sbin:/sbin \
    pnpm openclaw models list --json
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the real OpenClaw CLI after the patch:
  ```json
  {
    "count": 1,
    "models": [
      {
        "key": "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5",
        "name": "claude-sonnet-4-5",
        "input": "text+image",
        "contextWindow": 200000,
        "local": false,
        "available": false,
        "tags": [
          "default"
        ],
        "missing": false
      }
    ]
  }
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** The configured default Anthropic Claude 4 row now reports `input: "text+image"` instead of text-only, so the configured/default path preserves native image capability before request sanitization can drop image inputs.
- **What was not tested:** I did not make a live Anthropic API request. This proof covers the local configured model capability resolution and CLI-visible configured/default path that the reporter identified; the live provider call still depends on valid Anthropic credentials.

## Validation
- `pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/model.inline-provider.test.ts src/commands/models.list.e2e.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/model.inline-provider.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/model.inline-provider.test.ts src/commands/models/list.rows.ts src/commands/models.list.e2e.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Follow-up for https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/83756#issuecomment-4487709808.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR defects: source inspection shows the documented config is not accepted by the strict runtime schema, embedded system prompts do not receive `privacyConfig`, and session transcript writes are not connected to the encryption helper. For the broader feature, current main does not ship this privacy mode, so normal bug reproduction is not applicable.

target.title: feat(privacy): payload PII redaction and AES-256-GCM encryption at rest
target.body:
## Summary

- **PII redaction**: Detects and masks 6 categories (email, phone, SSN, credit card, IPv4, UUID) across tool outputs, user messages, TTS text, and system prompt context files. Zero-false-positive philosophy — prefers missing PII over corrupting valid technical content.
- **System prompt masking**: Optionally strips hostname, OS, shell, and repo path from the Runtime line; can suppress context file injection entirely.
- **Media blocking**: Drops image/audio/video attachments before they reach LLM providers when `privacy.media.blockAttachments` is enabled.
- **Session encryption at rest**: AES-256-GCM with PBKDF2-SHA256 key derivation (210k iterations). Per-line encryption of JSONL session files with independent IVs and auth tags. Atomic file writes to prevent corruption.
- **Plugin SDK subpath**: New `openclaw/plugin-sdk/privacy-runtime` exposes `redactPiiText` for extensions (used by speech-core TTS).

### Integration points

| Data path | Guard | Default |
|---|---|---|
| Tool output text | `privacy.pii.toolOutputs` | ON |
| Inbound user messages | `privacy.pii.userMessages` | OFF (opt-in) |
| TTS speech text | `privacy.pii.enabled` | ON |
| System prompt context files | `privacy.pii.systemPrompt` | ON |
| Media attachments | `privacy.media.blockAttachments` | OFF |
| Session files at rest | `privacy.atRest.enabled` | OFF |

### Configuration

```yaml
privacy:
  enabled: true
  pii:
    enabled: true
    systemPrompt: true
    userMessages: false       # opt-in — redacting user input is destructive
    toolOutputs: true
    categories:
      email: {}               # default placeholder [EMAIL]
      phone: { placeholder: "[PHONE_NUMBER]" }
      ssn: { redact: false }  # per-category opt-out
      creditCard: {}
      ipv4: {}
      uuid: {}
  media:
    blockAttachments: false
    warnOnBlock: true
  atRest:
    enabled: true
    passphrase: ${OPENCLAW_ENCRYPTION_KEY}
    pbkdf2Iterations: 210000
  systemPrompt:
    maskHostname: true
    maskRepoPath: true
    maskOs: false
    maskShell: false
    suppressContextFiles: false
```

### New files

- `src/privacy/types.ts` — `PrivacyConfig` type definition
- `src/privacy/payload-redact.ts` — PII detection and redaction engine
- `src/privacy/session-encrypt.ts` — AES-256-GCM session file encryption
- `src/plugin-sdk/privacy-runtime.ts` — SDK subpath for extensions
- `src/privacy/payload-redact.test.ts` — 20 unit tests
- `src/privacy/session-encrypt.test.ts` — 16 unit tests
- `src/privacy/inbound-redact.test.ts` — 13 integration tests

### Modified files

- `src/config/types.openclaw.ts` — added `privacy?: PrivacyConfig` to `OpenClawConfig`
- `src/agents/system-prompt.ts` — runtime line masking + context file redaction via `buildProjectContextSection`
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts` — tool output PII redaction
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.types.ts` — added `"config"` to `ToolHandlerParams`
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.ts` — media attachment blocking
- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts` — inbound user message redaction
- `extensions/speech-core/src/tts.ts` — TTS text PII redaction before speech providers
- `package.json` — registered `./plugin-sdk/privacy-runtime` export
- `scripts/lib/plugin-sdk-entrypoints.json` — added `privacy-runtime` entrypoint

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test src/privacy/` — 49 privacy tests pass (3 files: payload-redact, session-encrypt, inbound-redact)

target.title: feat: add per-agent compaction overrides (AI-assisted)
target.body:
## Summary

Adds per-agent compaction overrides so each configured agent can choose its own compaction runtime behavior instead of inheriting every compaction decision from `agents.defaults.compaction`.

This is intended for multi-agent installations where the main assistant, channel-specific agents, local/self-hosted agents, and Codex/app-server workers have different runtime and transcript-management requirements.

## Linked Issues And Related Work

Closes #52732

Related issues:

- #52751
- #69988
- #19501
- #15826
- #43834
- #6571
- #6572
- #30452
- #57901
- #58077
- #59863
- #60512
- #80784
- #82008

Related review/regression context addressed in this PR:

- strict `AgentEntrySchema` support for `agents.list[].compaction`
- defaults-only consumers for memory/notice/post-compaction behavior
- queued/direct compaction scope for legacy-session, non-main-agent runs

## Problem / Root Cause

Before this change, the config surface either rejected or only partially honored per-agent compaction settings. Several runtime paths still read from `agents.defaults.compaction` even when a specific `agents.list[]` entry defined its own compaction block.

That made these setups difficult or impossible to express correctly:

- main/default agent compacts with one provider/model
- channel or proxy agents compact with another provider/model
- local/self-hosted agents use a local provider alias for compaction
- Codex/app-server agents intentionally omit an OpenClaw compaction model override and keep their own runtime behavior
- non-main agents with legacy/non-agent session keys still need their active agent scope preserved during queued/direct compaction

## What Changed

This PR makes the per-agent compaction contract real end to end:

- extends the strict agent schema to accept `agents.list[].compaction`
- documents and labels the per-agent compaction fields
- resolves compaction provider/model/auth/profile choices through the active agent scope
- applies per-agent compaction settings in direct and queued compaction paths
- carries active `agentId` through preflight memory compaction, manual `/compact`, gateway session compaction, queued compaction, and direct compaction
- honors per-agent settings for:
  - `model`
  - `provider`
  - `authProfileId`
  - `timeoutSeconds`
  - `midTurnPrecheck`
  - `keepRecentTokens`
  - `truncateAfterCompaction`
  - `postIndexSync`
  - compaction memory-flush behavior
  - compaction follow-up/notice behavior
- keeps Codex/app-server agents able to omit a compaction model override so their native runtime path is not accidentally replaced by OpenClaw compaction selection
- adds regression coverage for legacy-session/non-main-agent compaction scope in both direct and queued compaction

## Reviewer Notes

The important behavioral invariant is:

> A compaction run should resolve configuration from the same active agent scope as the session/run that triggered it, even when the session key is legacy-shaped and does not encode `agent:<id>:`.

That is why this PR passes `agentId` explicitly into compaction params instead of relying only on parsing `sessionKey`.

The schema accepts the full per-agent compaction block, but this PR does not narrow the feature surface to schema-only support. The remaining defaults-only runtime consumers found during review were fixed so the accepted configuration is actually honored.

The PR is large because it is stacked on a broad branch, but the compaction-specific changes are intentionally scoped to config/schema/docs plus the runtime consumers needed to make the accepted per-agent fields real.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Per-agent compaction overrides must resolve from the active agent scope instead of always inheriting `agents.defaults.compaction`, including legacy/non-agent-shaped session keys that require an explicit non-main `agentId`.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on the PR branch plus a live OpenClaw gateway on `OpenClaw 2026.5.18 (50a2481)`, Node `v24.15.0`, host `Nano-01`, Discord channel binding for `#gb10`, native Codex app-server runtime authenticated through OAuth. Runtime proof logging was temporarily enabled at `/home/kklouzal/.openclaw/logs/gateway-debug-compaction-proof.log` with secrets redacted.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a live source-module proof with `HEAD_SHA=7e5f7acdde9b052c5b41213485380ee9c11e8a0d NODE_COMPILE_CACHE= node --import tsx .artifacts/per-agent-compaction-proof.mjs`; then verified the deployed multi-agent config on a real gateway and triggered manual `/compact` in the real Discord `#gb10` channel.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal transcript and redacted runtime log excerpt from the real setup:

```text
$ HEAD_SHA=7e5f7acdde9b052c5b41213485380ee9c11e8a0d NODE_COMPILE_CACHE= node --import tsx .artifacts/per-agent-compaction-proof.mjs
OpenClaw per-agent compaction live source proof
head=7e5f7acdde9b052c5b41213485380ee9c11e8a0d
sessionKey=legacy-topic-47
explicitAgentId=lossless-agent
resolvedSessionAgentId=lossless-agent
compactionProvider=google
compactionModel=gemini-3.1-pro-preview
timeoutMs=45000
truncateAfterCompaction=true
result=PASS: legacy session kept non-main agent compaction override
```

```json
{
  "liveConfig": {
    "main": { "compaction": { "model": "openai/gpt-5.4-mini" } },
    "webproxy": { "compaction": { "model": "openai/gpt-5.4-mini" } },
    "gb10-gemma": { "compaction": { "model": "vllm-gemma/gb10-live" } },
    "codex-worker": { "compaction": null }
  },
  "liveSession": {
    "sessionKey": "agent:main:discord:channel:1487999596876533801",
    "sessionId": "00109ec6-e729-4d21-9811-8294ad845acb",
    "status": "done",
    "model": "gpt-5.5",
    "compactionCount": 1,
    "totalTokens": 59817,
    "codexThreadId": "019e3eb3-e62c-7053-972f-3f43962ec946",
    "authProfileId": "openai-codex:<redacted>",
    "codexSidecarUpdatedAt": "2026-05-19T05:27:39.290Z"
  }
}
```

```text
2026-05-18T22:27:13.204-07:00 DEBUG preflightCompaction check: sessionKey=agent:main:discord:channel:1487999596876533801 tokenCount=undefined contextWindow=200000 threshold=136800 isHeartbeat=false isCli=false persistedFresh=false transcriptPromptTokens=undefined promptTokensEst=2 activeTranscriptBytes=undefined maxActiveTranscriptBytes=8388608 sizeTrigger=false
2026-05-18T22:27:13.224-07:00 DEBUG memoryFlush check: sessionKey=agent:main:discord:channel:1487999596876533801 tokenCount=undefined contextWindow=200000 threshold=136800 isHeartbeat=false isCli=false memoryFlushWritable=true compactionCount=0 memoryFlushCompactionCount=undefined persistedPromptTokens=undefined persistedFresh=false promptTokensEst=2 transcriptPromptTokens=undefined transcriptOutputTokens=undefined projectedTokenCount=undefined transcriptBytes=undefined forceFlushTranscriptBytes=2097152 forceFlushByTranscriptSize=false
2026-05-18T22:27:15.034-07:00 DEBUG agent harness selected sessionKey=agent:main:discord:channel:1487999596876533801 agentId=main selectedHarnessId=codex runtime=codex provider=openai-codex modelId=gpt-5.5
2026-05-18T22:28:03.337-07:00 INFO  started codex app-server compaction sessionId=00109ec6-e729-4d21-9811-8294ad845acb
2026-05-18T22:28:31.302-07:00 INFO  completed codex app-server compaction sessionId=00109ec6-e729-4d21-9811-8294ad845acb
```
- Observed result after fix: The source-module proof selected the explicit non-main per-agent compaction override values `google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview`, `45000` ms timeout, and transcript rotation enabled for a legacy session key. The live deployed OpenClaw run accepted distinct per-agent compaction config blocks and completed a real Discord `/compact` in `#gb10`; the persisted session shows `compactionCount: 1`, `status: "done"`, and a live Codex app-server sidecar thread updated during the run.
- What was not tested: The live deployed Codex app-server compaction log does not currently print the resolved per-agent compaction model, so the live `#gb10` run is supplemental runtime proof while the source-module proof is the direct model/provider/timeout selection proof. Existing pre-restart Codex app-server sessions can also fail manual `/compact` with stale/missing native thread IDs; that appears to be a separate restart/session-recovery behavior and is not claimed fixed here.

## Supplemental Proof / Validation

Focused regression and runtime-scope tests:

```text
corepack pnpm test \
 src/agents/agent-scope.test.ts \
 src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.compaction-safety-timeout.test.ts \
 src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts \

 src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.memory-flush-forwarding.test.ts \
 src/auto-reply/reply/memory-flush.test.ts \
 src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts \
 src/auto-reply/reply/commands-compact.test.ts
```

Result:

target.title: docs(context-engine): add OpenViking as remote context-engine example
target.body:
Add OpenViking as an additional context-engine plugin example alongside lossless-claw in the Quick Start section.

## Changes

- Add a **From ClawHub** install tab showing OpenViking's ClawHub install path
- Add a remote engine config example (OpenViking) alongside the existing local engine example (lossless-claw)

## Why

The current Quick Start only shows one third-party engine (lossless-claw, local SQLite). OpenViking is a remote context-engine plugin that implements the full ContextEngine interface (ingest, assemble, compact, afterTurn) with long-term memory, session archive, and semantic retrieval over a separately deployed server. Adding it gives users a reference for the remote engine pattern.

- ClawHub: @openviking/openclaw-plugin (latest: 2026.5.8)
- GitHub: https://github.com/volcengine/OpenViking (24K+ stars)
- npm: @openviking/openclaw-plugin

target.title: fix(cron): use structured denial signals
target.body:
## Summary

- Remove cron outcome classification based on denial tokens found in summary, output text, final assistant text, or payload prose.
- Preserve fatal cron denial handling through typed `failureSignal` metadata sourced from structured tool error codes.
- Preserve thrown gateway/node-host denial codes across the embedded tool lifecycle catch path, including wrapped `UNAVAILABLE` gateway errors whose nested `nodeError.message` starts with a canonical denial token.
- Add regression coverage so prose-only denial text is not promoted to fatal, while structured denial signals still are.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: cron diagnostic classification no longer treats ordinary assistant/tool prose containing denial-looking phrases as a fatal cron failure unless structured error metadata marks the run as fatal. The follow-up patch also covers thrown gateway errors from the real lifecycle catch path, where `GatewayClientRequestError.gatewayCode`, nested `details.nodeError.code`, and nested `details.nodeError.message` must survive conversion into `tool_execution_end`.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout in an isolated worktree on this branch, using the patched embedded tool lifecycle and cron failure-signal modules from the worktree root.

Exact steps or command run after the patch:

```sh
node --import tsx -e '<build a thrown gateway/node-host denial, convert it through buildToolLifecycleErrorResult, extract the structured code, and resolve the cron failure signal>'
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.tools.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/failure-signal.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent.helpers.test.ts
pnpm tsgo:test:src
pnpm check:changed
git diff --check
```

Evidence after fix: terminal output from the one-shot runtime probe:

```json
{
  "lifecycleStatus": "error",
  "wrapperCode": "UNAVAILABLE",
  "nestedNodeCode": "UNAVAILABLE",
  "nestedNodeMessage": "SYSTEM_RUN_DENIED: approval required",
  "extractedErrorCode": "SYSTEM_RUN_DENIED",
  "fatalForCron": true,
  "failureSignalCode": "SYSTEM_RUN_DENIED",
  "failureSignalMessage": "UNAVAILABLE: SYSTEM_RUN_DENIED: approval required"
}
```

Observed result after fix: prose-only `SYSTEM_RUN_DENIED` / `could not run` text remains normal cron output, prose-only tool output does not synthesize an error code, typed structured denial metadata still produces a fatal cron failure, and thrown gateway/node-host denials now normalize the production shape where both wrapper and nested codes are `UNAVAILABLE` while the nested message starts with `SYSTEM_RUN_DENIED`.

What was not tested: full baseline `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test` was not run; this used the focused core lane for the touched classifier and tool-error lifecycle surfaces. No production cron job was modified.

Supplemental checks:

- Focused Vitest lane passed: 6 files, 145 tests.
- `pnpm tsgo:test:src` passed.
- `pnpm check:changed` passed the core/coreTests lane, lint, typechecks, dependency guards, import-cycle guard, and related changed-file checks.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- `docs/automation/cron-jobs.md` was updated so the documented cron contract matches the structured-only classifier plus node-host wrapper compatibility.

## Review

`codex review --base origin/main` was attempted twice locally, but the local Codex CLI failed before review started with OpenAI API 401 auth errors. Manual review caught and fixed one extraction bug before opening the PR, and the ClawSweeper/Codex review finding about thrown gateway errors is addressed in the latest commit.

## AI-assisted

This PR was AI-assisted. I reviewed the diff, understand the code path, and ran the proof above locally.

target.title: fix: rebuild legacy flow_runs tables
target.body:
## Summary

Fix a TaskFlow SQLite migration bug that can break real webhook-triggered agent actions on upgraded installs.

On affected installs, the `flow_runs` table can be left in a legacy hybrid state where SQLite still enforces `owner_session_key TEXT NOT NULL` even though newer code writes only `owner_key`.

That means webhook-triggered `create_flow` can fail at runtime with:

`NOT NULL constraint failed: flow_runs.owner_session_key`

This change rebuilds legacy `flow_runs` tables when `owner_session_key` is still present, normalizing them to the current schema so flow creation works again.

## What changed

- add a `rebuildLegacyFlowRunsTable()` helper in the TaskFlow registry SQLite store
- preserve existing rows while mapping legacy data into the current schema
- remove `owner_session_key` from rebuilt tables and enforce `owner_key NOT NULL`
- keep coverage for legacy and hybrid upgraded database shapes

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tasks/task-flow-registry.store.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Upgraded SQLite stores with legacy `owner_session_key TEXT NOT NULL` must migrate to the current `owner_key` schema and accept new managed flow writes.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on Linux, Node `v24.15.0`, temp `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, real TaskFlow registry/store modules, and Node SQLite.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node --import tsx --input-type=module <one-off-proof-script.mjs>
```

Evidence after fix: Redacted terminal output:

```json
{
  "restored": {
    "flowId": "legacy-flow",
    "ownerKey": "agent:test:legacy",
    "syncMode": "managed",
    "controllerId": "core/legacy-restored",
    "revision": 0,
    "status": "queued"
  },
  "created": {
    "ownerKey": "agent:test:fresh",
    "syncMode": "managed",
    "controllerId": "proof/migrated-flow",
    "status": "queued"
  },
  "hasOwnerSessionKey": false,
  "ownerKeyNotNull": 1,
  "rows": [
    { "flow_id": "legacy-flow", "owner_key": "agent:test:legacy", "sync_mode": "managed", "controller_id": "core/legacy-restored", "revision": 0, "status": "queued" },
    { "owner_key": "agent:test:fresh", "sync_mode": "managed", "controller_id": "proof/migrated-flow", "revision": 0, "status": "queued" }
  ]
}
```

Observed result after fix: The legacy row restored with `owner_key`, the rebuilt table no longer had `owner_session_key`, `owner_key` was `NOT NULL`, and a new managed flow inserted successfully after migration.

What was not tested: A full webhook HTTP round trip was not run; this proof exercises the real persistence path that webhook `create_flow` uses when creating managed TaskFlows.

target.body:
## Summary

Security audit identified 4 HIGH/CRITICAL vulnerabilities. This PR fixes all of them with minimal, targeted changes.

### 1. Skill Scanner Bypass (CRITICAL) — `src/security/skill-scanner.ts`
All 9 existing scanner rules are bypassable via basic JavaScript obfuscation (dynamic property access, import aliasing, prototype pollution, indirect eval). 

**Fix:** Add 6 new LINE_RULES and 3 new SOURCE_RULES:
- `dangerous-exec-dynamic`: detects `cp["exec"]()` bracket notation
- `dangerous-exec-indirect`: detects `Reflect.apply`, `Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor`
- `dynamic-code-execution-indirect`: detects `globalThis[...]`, `.constructor.constructor`
- `prototype-pollution`: detects `__proto__`, `Object.prototype.x =`
- `import-aliasing`: detects `{ exec: aliasName }` destructuring
- Unicode escape sequences, `String.fromCharCode`, dynamic `import()` of dangerous modules
- Lower base64 threshold from 200→100 chars, hex threshold from 6→4 sequences
- Expand network API coverage: `http.get`, `XMLHttpRequest`, `WebSocket`, `axios`

### 2. SSRF via Unguarded fetch() (HIGH) — `src/plugins/provider-self-hosted-setup.ts`
`discoverOpenAICompatibleLocalModels()` uses raw `fetch()` with user-provided `baseUrl`, enabling SSRF to cloud metadata endpoints (169.254.169.254), internal services, and API key leakage.

**Fix:** Replace `fetch()` with `fetchWithSsrFGuard()` using `allowPrivateNetwork: true` (needed for legitimate local providers) and proper dispatcher release.

### 3. Hook Priority TOCTOU (CRITICAL) — `src/plugins/registry.ts`
No upper bound on hook `priority` allows malicious plugins to register at priority 9999, running before security-critical hooks to auto-approve dangerous tool calls, inject prompts, or tamper transcripts.

**Fix:** Cap priority for external plugins at 100 and bundled plugins at 1000. Log diagnostic warning when priority is clamped.

### 4. Device Token Revocation TOCTOU (HIGH) — `src/infra/device-pairing.ts`
`verifyDeviceToken()` checks `revokedAtMs` early but mutates state later. A concurrent `revokeDeviceToken()` can revoke the token between check and state mutation.

**Fix:** Re-check `revokedAtMs` immediately before `lastUsedAtMs` update to narrow the TOCTOU window.

## PoC Documents

Detailed proof-of-concept documents with exploit code are included in `security-pocs/`:
- `01-skill-scanner-bypass.md` — 5 complete bypass techniques
- `02-ssrf-unguarded-fetch.md` — 4 SSRF attack scenarios
- `03-hook-priority-toctou.md` — 4 hook manipulation attacks
- `04-device-token-toctou.md` — Race condition timeline

## Test plan

- [ ] Verify `pnpm test src/security/skill-scanner.test.ts` passes with new rules
- [ ] Verify `pnpm test src/plugins` passes with hook priority capping
- [ ] Verify self-hosted provider discovery still works with SSRF guard
- [ ] Verify device token verification/revocation under concurrent load
- [ ] Review PoC documents for accuracy

target.title: fix(channels): suppress late raw tool output
target.body:
## Summary
- Keeps failed tool-call status visible through the compact progress/item callbacks for message-tool-only channel replies, while suppressing raw text-only failed tool-result payloads that were escaping as separate messages.
- Stops Telegram and Discord progress drafts/tool payloads from accepting late text-only updates after the final answer has been delivered.
- Preserves media tool results and exec approval payloads so real attachment/approval flows are not hidden by the suppression.

## Root Cause
Before/RCA proof:
- Parent-source proof: before this patch, `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts` suppressed default tool progress only when `!hasMedia && !hasExecApproval && deliveryPayload.isError !== true`. Exact inspection command: `git show HEAD^:src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts | sed -n '1615,1675p'`. That condition explicitly let text-only failed tool results bypass the compact-progress suppression path.
- Regression trigger proof: `57da466ecbb5f63b5a1881ae8caa7359c02ec40c` (`Fix Discord verbose tool progress delivery (#80042)`, merged May 17, 2026) changed source-suppressed verbose progress from direct-chat-only to `sourceReplyDeliveryMode === "message_tool_only"`. That correctly restored compact verbose progress for channel replies, but it also made the older `isError` bypass visible in Telegram/Discord message-tool-only turns.
- Late-tail proof: Discord's generic fallback final-delivery branch marked the reply sent but did not mark the draft preview as final-delivered, while Telegram's tool/progress paths accepted text-only tool updates after the answer lane was finalized. That let asynchronous failed command output update or restart progress after the final chat message.
- Scope proof: the leak is shared dispatch/subscription behavior plus Telegram and Discord adapter finalization, not a Telegram-only issue.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Failed tool calls still appear in the compact progress state, but raw failed tool-output text no longer dumps after the final Telegram/Discord channel reply.

Real environment tested: Local tmp worktree branch `fix-channel-late-tool-output` at `bf0679884daa` rebased onto `origin/main` `d0f7c8fa280534c3be786fd1ec69b569bbd72200`; private Telegram/Discord identifiers from the reported session were intentionally not included in this public PR.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.draft-preview.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-pi-session.suppresses-message-end-block-replies-message-tool.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.types.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts`
- `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-pi-session.suppresses-message-end-block-replies-message-tool.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`

Evidence after fix:
- `oxfmt`: `All matched files use the correct format. Finished in 45ms on 13 files using 1 threads.`
- `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`: no output.
- Vitest: `Test Files 6 passed (6); Tests 398 passed (398); Duration 13.12s`.
- New shared dispatch coverage proves `onCommandOutput` still receives the compact failed `exec` status while raw `sendToolResult` does not receive `raw failed command output` in `message_tool_only` mode.
- New embedded subscription coverage proves text-only tool summaries after committed message-tool delivery are suppressed.
- New Discord coverage proves the progress draft shows the pre-final compact `Exec` state and is not updated by late failed command output after final delivery.
- New Telegram coverage proves post-final text-only tool payloads are suppressed and command-output callbacks do not restart progress drafts after the final answer.

Observed result after fix: Compact failed-tool progress remains available before/finalizing the answer, and late raw text-only failed tool payloads are dropped after final delivery; media payloads and exec approval payloads remain deliverable.

What was not tested: Live Telegram/Discord E2E posting with real accounts was not run from this tmp worktree to avoid touching private reported channels. The requested Codex review pass was attempted with both `codex review --uncommitted` and the skill wrapper; both failed before diff inspection with local `bwrap: loopback: Failed RTM_NEWADDR: Operation not permitted`, so no review finding was accepted as proof.

## Verification
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 <13 touched files>`
- `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-pi-session.suppresses-message-end-block-replies-message-tool.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`

## What was not tested
Live Telegram/Discord E2E with real accounts/channels was not run; this PR uses source-level RCA plus focused shared, Discord, and Telegram regression tests as the proof path.

target.title: fix(acp): fail fast on dead spawns and recover stale disconnects
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2-5 bullets:

If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(<plugin-id>): beta blocker - <summary>` and link the matching `Beta blocker: <plugin-name> - <summary>` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation.

- Problem: ACP sessions could report a successful `/acp spawn` even when the newly initialized runtime handle was already unusable, and stale gateway disconnects could leave cancel/close/early-turn recovery stuck on a bad cached handle.
- Why it matters: Operators can see contradictory UX (`Spawned` followed by immediate `ACP_TURN_FAILED`) and then lose reliable recovery controls for the affected ACP session.
- What changed: Probe the freshly initialized runtime status before reporting spawn success; clean up failed post-spawn sessions; treat explicit stale gateway disconnect close codes as recoverable for close, cancel, active-turn cancel, and early-turn retry.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No ACP protocol/schema changes, no new public commands, no auth/provider changes, no gateway restart policy changes, and no broad retry policy for non-stale gateway errors.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes # (none)
- Related #63656
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`.

- Root cause: Spawn success was emitted after `initializeSession` without verifying the new runtime handle could answer an immediate status check, and the ACP manager only treated `acpx exited ...` as a stale runtime-handle recovery signal.
- Missing detection / guardrail: There was no spawn-time liveliness guard, no regression for gateway-disconnect stale-handle recovery, and no active-turn cancel test for stale disconnect cleanup.
- Contributing context (if known): ACP transport disconnects surface as `Gateway disconnected: 1006` or `gateway closed (...)`; some close codes indicate stale transport, while others such as `1008` represent real authorization/pairing failures.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should catch this. Otherwise write `N/A`.

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts`
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-acp.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in:
  - `/acp spawn` fails and cleans up when the immediate status probe reports `Gateway disconnected: 1006`.
  - Spawn health checks call direct runtime status and do not depend on capability lookup.
  - Close, passive cancel, active-turn cancel, and early-turn retry recover known stale gateway disconnect close codes.
  - Non-stale gateway closures such as `1008 pairing required` are surfaced instead of swallowed.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The behavior is localized to ACP command lifecycle and `AcpSessionManager` runtime-handle recovery, so mocked runtime tests can deterministically exercise every branch without a live ACP backend.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing ACP manager tests covered equivalent `acpx exited ...` recovery and normal active-turn cancel; this PR extends that pattern to gateway disconnect errors and post-spawn health checks.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- `/acp spawn` no longer reports success when the freshly initialized ACP runtime immediately fails status with a gateway disconnect; it returns the ACP error and cleans up the failed session.
- `/acp cancel` and `/acp close` can recover stale gateway-disconnected runtime handles for explicit stale/disconnect close codes.
- First ACP turns that fail immediately with a stale gateway disconnect get one fresh-handle retry.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
/acp spawn -> initializeSession OK -> "Spawned" -> first prompt -> ACP_TURN_FAILED
           -> cancel/close can stay stuck on stale cached handle

After:
/acp spawn -> initializeSession OK -> direct runtime status probe
            -> fail? cleanup + ACP error
            -> pass? bind/report spawn success

stale gateway disconnect during close/cancel/early first turn
  -> clear cached handle -> recover or retry once
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS local checkout
- Runtime/container: Node.js via repo `pnpm` lanes
- Model/provider: ACP runtime mocked in regression tests
- Integration/channel (if any): ACP slash command lifecycle
- Relevant config (redacted): ACP enabled with `acpx` backend

### Steps

1. Spawn an ACP session while the freshly initialized runtime status path fails with `Gateway disconnected: 1006`.
2. Attempt cancel/close against an ACP session whose cached runtime handle is stale from a gateway disconnect.
3. Attempt an early first turn that fails before output with `Gateway disconnected: 1006`.

### Expected

- Spawn fails immediately with cleanup instead of reporting false success.
- Recoverable stale disconnects clear the cached handle and allow recovery/fresh-handle retry.
- Non-stale gateway errors remain visible.

### Actual

- Before this fix, spawn could report success before immediate turn failure, and stale gateway disconnects were not covered by the existing stale-runtime recovery handling.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Validation:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config vitest.config.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-acp.test.ts`
  - `3` files passed, `166` tests passed.
- `pnpm check:changed`
  - Stopped during the final broad e2e shard because the local changed-gate plan selected 293 targets. Completed before stop: core typecheck, core test typecheck, lint, import-cycle checks, gateway shard (`91` files / `990` tests), auto-reply shard (`38` files / `494` tests), agents shard (`41` files / `746` tests), and the smaller ACP/plugin/CLI/commands shards shown in local output.

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - Spawn-health failure returns the ACP error, avoids binding, closes the runtime, and deletes the failed session.
  - Spawn-health success path does not call capability lookup.
  - Stale gateway disconnect recovery clears cached handles for close, passive cancel, active-turn cancel, and early-turn retry.
- Edge cases checked:
  - Recoverable gateway disconnects are limited to explicit stale/disconnect close codes.
  - `1008 pairing required` is not treated as recoverable.
- What you did **not** verify:
  - Full live ACP backend replay in this PR cycle.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`.

- Risk: A post-spawn status probe can reject a session that is already unstable immediately after initialization.
  - Mitigation: This is intentional fail-fast behavior to avoid false-success spawns; failed sessions are cleaned up.
- Risk: Gateway disconnect recovery could hide real gateway failures if classified too broadly.
  - Mitigation: Recovery is limited to an explicit stale/disconnect close-code allowlist, with negative tests for `1008 pairing required`.

target.title: fix(image): mixed-source image input for vision-capable models
target.body:
## Summary - Problem: In WebUI, when a prompt mixes an uploaded inline image with an explicit HTTP(S) image URL, only the inline image is included in the first native vision request. The HTTP(S) URL remains plain text and is effectively ignored unless the model later calls the fallback `image` tool. - Why it matters: Mixed-source image prompts behave inconsistently. URL-only prompts can appear to work, but once an inline image is also present the remote image is silently dropped from native vision input. - What changed: `detectImageReferences()` now recognizes explicit HTTP(S) image URLs as prompt image refs, `detectAndLoadPromptImages()` preloads them through `loadWebMedia(...)`, and the runtime merges them into the same native `images` payload as inline/offloaded images for vision-capable models. Focused regression coverage was also added in `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/images.test.ts`. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): This PR does not change fallback `image` tool exposure, non-vision model behavior, non-WebUI channels, or local-path/sandbox access rules. It also does not add new user-facing error messaging when a referenced image fails to load. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [x] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes #62514 - Related #62514 - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Root Cause - Root cause: `detectImageReferences()` only handled local paths, `file://` URLs, and `media://inbound/...` claim-check refs. Explicit HTTP(S) image URLs were intentionally ignored, so mixed turns preloaded only inline/offloaded attachments into native vision input. - Missing detection / guardrail: We did not have focused coverage asserting that a mixed inline + HTTP(S) URL prompt produces a single first-turn native vision payload containing both images. - Contributing context (if known): HTTP(S)-only prompts could still work when the runtime later fell back to the `image` tool, which masked the issue and made the behavior look model-dependent. ## User-visible / Behavior Changes - WebUI prompts that mix uploaded inline images with explicit HTTP(S) image URLs now send both images to the first native vision request for vision-capable models. - Explicit HTTP(S) image URLs no longer depend on the model deciding to call the fallback `image` tool when other image sources are present. - Existing behavior for non-vision models and the fallback tool path is unchanged. ## Repro + Verification ### Steps 1. Open WebUI and select a vision-capable model. 2. Upload an inline image and include an explicit HTTP(S) image URL in the same prompt. 3. Ask the model to describe both images. ### Expected - Both the uploaded inline image and the explicit HTTP(S) image URL are available to the same first native vision request. ### Actual - Before this change, only the inline image was included in the first native vision request. - The HTTP(S) URL stayed as plain text and was ignored unless the model later called the fallback `image` tool. ## Evidence ![img_v3_0210j_08150ab9-cec6-4536-b968-da852653febg](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d1f89d69-fa63-469b-a1cf-95262f2b4fad) Attach at least one: - [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after - [ ] Trace/log snippets - [x] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - Verified scenarios: WebUI with a vision-capable model using mixed inline + HTTP(S) image inputs; HTTP(S)-only image prompts; non-vision model behavior remains unchanged. - Edge cases checked: duplicate refs are deduped; explicit remote URLs are preloaded together with inline/offloaded images; non-image remote responses are skipped instead of being forwarded as images. - What you did **not** verify: non-WebUI channels / other chat entry points. ## Diagram ### Before ```mermaid flowchart TD A["Vision model
single URL image"] --> B["image-tools"] B --> C["default image model"] C --> D["vision model"] D --> E["2 model calls"] F["Vision model
inline image + URL image"] --> G["first native vision request"] G --> H["inline image goes into
native images"] G --> I["URL image stays as
plain text"] I --> J["URL image is ignored"] H --> K["vision model gets
incomplete image context"] J --> K ``` ### After ```mermaid flowchart TD A["Vision model
single URL image"] --> B["extract URL image"] B --> C["put into native image input"] C --> D["vision model"] D --> E["1 model call
no tool path"] F["Vision model
inline image + URL image"] --> G["extract URL image"] G --> H["merge URL image with
inline image"] H --> I["put into native images"] I --> J["vision model sees
all images"] ```

target.title: fix(compaction): guard malformed token estimation
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: long-lived `main` sessions could crash before provider dispatch when compaction token estimation hit malformed replay history and `estimateTokens()` read missing `.length` fields.
- Why it matters: once a session contained one malformed history block, every later prompt attempt could fail in pre-prompt compaction, making the session effectively unrecoverable.
- What changed: added a guarded `estimateMessageTokens()` path in `src/agents/compaction.ts` and switched preemptive compaction plus embedded compaction metrics/sanity checks to reuse it.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this PR does not redesign replay-history normalization or patch `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent`; it only hardens OpenClaw’s local compaction estimation path.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #63612
- This likely also addresses the malformed-history / `reading 'length'` Telegram manifestation discussed in #64053, and may partially reduce the Telegram crash facet mentioned in #64034, but it does not address the broader Discord overflow/lag symptoms tracked there.

- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: compaction-side token estimation assumed replayed message blocks always had fully normalized shapes, but malformed assistant/toolResult blocks could still reach estimation and trigger unchecked `.length` reads.
- Missing detection / guardrail: OpenClaw had replay sanitization and some downstream try/catch sites, but no shared safe estimator for all compaction-related `estimateTokens()` call sites.
- Contributing context (if known): long-lived `main` sessions exercise pre-prompt compaction on every turn, so one malformed history block could repeatedly fail the recovery path itself.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/agents/compaction.test.ts`, `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: malformed assistant/toolResult history blocks do not throw during token estimation or pre-prompt compaction checks.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the crash happens in pure estimation logic before provider dispatch, so unit coverage at the estimation and precheck seam is enough to lock in the failure mode.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Long-lived sessions with malformed replay history now fail soft in compaction token estimation instead of crashing before reply generation.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[new user turn] -> [pre-prompt compaction estimation] -> [throws on malformed block] -> [session cannot reply]

After:
[new user turn] -> [guarded token estimation] -> [invalid block counted as 0/safe fallback] -> [reply flow continues]
```
## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)

target.title: fix: accept valid MCP tool schemas without strict inputSchema.type check
target.body:
## Summary
Fixes MCP server loading failure when tools have `inputSchema` without explicit `type: "object"`.

Closes #63602

## Changes
- Replace `client.listTools()` with `client.request()` using a lenient Zod schema (`LenientListToolsResultSchema`) that accepts `inputSchema` as any record rather than requiring `type: z.literal("object")`.
- Add `normalizeInputSchema()` to backfill `type: "object"` only when the field is missing, preserving any explicit type the server provides.
- Add `isValidRootInputSchema()` and a catalog-loop gate that skips tools whose root `inputSchema.type` is explicitly non-object (e.g. `"string"`, `"array"`) and emits a `logWarn`. This stops malformed schemas from reaching provider tool payloads where they could fail the entire request on OpenAI-style function-call routes.
- CHANGELOG entry under Unreleased / Fixes.

## Root Cause
The MCP SDK v1.29.0 `ToolSchema` validates `inputSchema.type` as `z.literal("object")`. Some MCP servers (especially older SDKs or custom implementations) omit `type` from `inputSchema`, returning `{ properties: { ... } }`, which trips a Zod validation error: `invalid_value expected "object" in inputSchema.type`.

## Testing
- `LenientListToolsResultSchema.parse()` regression test for issue #63602: a `tools/list` payload whose `inputSchema` omits `type` now parses cleanly.
- Unit test for `normalizeInputSchema()`: backfills `type: "object"` only when missing; explicit non-object types pass through unchanged.
- Unit test for `isValidRootInputSchema()`: rejects explicit non-object root types (`"string"`, `"array"`, `"number"`).
- Full `vitest.agents.config.ts` suite passes locally on the rebased branch.
- TypeScript compilation (`pnpm tsgo:core`) and lint (`pnpm exec oxlint`) clean.
- The fix is backward-compatible: servers that already return `type: "object"` are unaffected.

## Review history
- v1 introduced lenient parsing + normalize.
- v2 fixed `normalizeInputSchema` to only backfill when `type` is missing (Greptile / Codex P2).
- v3 (this push) addresses the still-open Codex P1 by filtering tools with explicit non-object root schemas at catalog time, with a `logWarn` for visibility, and adds a regression test that exercises `LenientListToolsResultSchema` directly with a type-less payload.

---
*This PR was generated with AI assistance (Claude).*

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Bundle MCP tool cataloging accepts tools whose root `inputSchema` omits `type`, backfills `type: "object"`, skips tools with explicit non-object root schemas, and reports each server's `toolCount` from accepted catalog tools only.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS from PR head `0f05948d33`, Node `25.2.1`, pnpm `10.33.2`, running the bundle MCP runtime checks from this branch.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran `pnpm test src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-tools.materialize.test.ts`, `pnpm tsgo:core`, and `pnpm check:changed` in the PR worktree after rebasing onto current `main`.
- Evidence after fix: Console output excerpts from the local OpenClaw worktree:

```text
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  4 passed (4)

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  15 passed (15)
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 33.18s

> openclaw@2026.5.4 tsgo:core /Users/yossi.eliaz/openclaw-pr63634
> node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo

[check:changed] lanes=core, coreTests, docs
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
runtime-sidecar-loaders: local runtime sidecar loaders look OK.
Import cycle check: 0 runtime value cycle(s).
```

- Observed result after fix: The focused runtime tests passed, including the regression that only the accepted typeless tool remains in the catalog after filtering while explicit non-object root schemas are skipped, and local core type/lint/import-cycle guards passed.
- What was not tested: A live third-party MCP server connected to an actual model-provider tool call was not tested in this environment.

target.title: fix(config): strip legacy installs/plugins from channel configs before validation
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #63101

After upgrading from v4.5 to v4.8, `openclaw gateway restart` fails with:

```
channels.feishu: invalid config: must NOT have additional properties
```

when legacy `installs` and `plugins` fields are present under `channels.feishu`.

## Root Cause

The Feishu channel config schema (`FeishuConfigSchema`) uses Zod `.strict()` mode, which rejects any properties not explicitly declared in the schema. The `installs` and `plugins` fields were never valid channel-level properties but could appear in v4.5-era configs due to top-level key nesting.

## Fix

Add a legacy config migration (`channels.*.stale-top-level-keys`) that auto-strips `installs` and `plugins` from every `channels.*` entry (including nested `accounts.*`) **before** schema validation runs. The migration:

- Logs a clear change message so operators know the fields were removed
- Applies generically to all channels (not just Feishu) since these fields are never valid at the channel level
- Includes a legacy rule that triggers a warning for `channels.feishu` specifically

## Tests

- 6 regression tests for the migration in `src/config/legacy-migrate.test.ts`
- 3 schema-level rejection tests in `extensions/feishu/src/config-schema.test.ts`

All 51 tests pass.
